channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinians, Israel disagree on whether Gaza's crucial Rafah crossing will reopen Monday
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The Rafah border crossing between Gaza and Egypt will reopen Monday for people returning to Gaza, the Palestinian embassy in Egypt said Saturday, but the territory's sole gateway to the outside world will remain closed to people trying to leave.
item.dc:creator: TOQA EZZIDIN, SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky says Trump didn’t say ‘yes’ to Tomahawk missiles request
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday said President Trump poured cold water on his request to provide the Eastern European nation with Tomahawk missiles amid its over three-years-long war with Russia. “It's very difficult just to operate only with Ukrainian drones.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: One scandal too many forces UK monarchy to sideline Prince Andrew after years of tabloid fodder
item.description: Prince Andrew has been banished from the royal whirl after emails emerged this week showing that he had remained in contact with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein longer than he previously admitted.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: From barracks to palace: Soldiers who led military coups to become state leaders
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — Weeks of nationwide Gen Z protests in Madagascar sparked by power and water shortages escalated and led to a military coup that forced President Andry Rajoelina into exile. Army officer Col. Michael Randrianirina has been sworn in as the new leader of the Indian Ocean nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE GUMEDE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya, as thousands mourn former Prime Minister Raila Odinga
item.description: Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu before the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Israelis and Palestinians wait anxiously to see what comes after a ceasefire in the two-year war in Gaza, the experience of the Northern Ireland peace process in the 1990s may offer lessons in the thorny process of moving from seemingly intractable conflict to enduring peace.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel received coffin of missing hostage handed over to Red Cross, prime minister's office says
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel received the remains of another hostage from Gaza on Friday, the prime minister’s office said, a handover from Hamas as the militant group worked to shore up a ceasefire by using bulldozers in a search for bodies under the rubble in the war-scarred enclave.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump-Zelensky meeting
item.description: President Trump hosted Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday at the White House, part of a renewed push to end Russia’s more than three-year war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: EL ALTO, Bolivia (AP) — After nearly two decades of one-party rule, three years of an accelerating currency crisis and too many months of mind-numbing fuel lines, Bolivia is lurching to the right.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and CARLOS VALDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators look to block Trump from engaging in ‘hostilities’ in Venezuela
item.description: Senators Tim Kaine and Rand Paul lead bipartisan effort to prevent President Donald Trump from launching military strikes in Venezuela without Congress approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump's authorization of covert CIA strikes in Venezuela raises concerns of regime change and potential blowback from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: King to pray with pope in first since 1534
item.description: VATICAN CITY/LONDON, Oct 17 (Reuters) - Britain's King Charles and Queen Camilla will meet Pope Leo at the Vatican next week in a visit seen as a sign of closening ties between the Catholic Church and the Anglican Communion, five centuries after their turbulent separation.
item.dc:creator: Joshua McElwee and Muvija M, NewsNation

item.title: Russia convicts 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges
item.description: A court in southern Russia convicted 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges Friday after a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Japanese Prime Minister Murayama, known for apology over wartime aggression, dies at 101
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s former Prime Minister Tomiichi Murayama, who was known for his 1995 “Murayama statement” apologizing to Asian victims of his country’s aggression, died Friday. He was 101.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expresses optimism for Ukraine deal as Zelenskyy arrives in DC
item.description: “We need Tomahawks for the United States of America too,” Trump said. “We have a lot of them, but we need them. I mean, we can’t deplete our country.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signals new trust in Erdogan, raising concerns over Turkey’s ambitions in Gaza and beyond
item.description: Turkey seeks expanded influence in Gaza through construction contracts and peacekeeping roles as President Recep Tayyip Erdogan rebuilds trust with Washington after the ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department aware of reports after American tourists attacked, 1 killed in popular seaside destination
item.description: State Department aware of reports from coastal town near Lisbon, Portugal, as Cascais police investigate after two Americans attacked, leaving one dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO allies agree to increase weapon deliveries to Ukraine
item.description: Over half of NATO allies are now part of the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List, a program to buy U.S. weapons for the Eastern European nation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Trump confirms CIA authorization for operations in Venezuela
item.description: It is unclear whether any operations are planned or if the authorization serves as a contingency measure.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey deports peaceful Christians under guise of ‘national security’ claims watchdog
item.description: Human rights advocate warns Turkey is systematically targeting Christians for deportation, with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's policies facing international scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian officials meet with US weapons manufacturers before Trump-Zelenskyy talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian government delegation has met with prominent American weapons manufacturers during a U.S. visit, a senior Kyiv official said Wednesday, before President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s meeting with U.S. counterpart Donald Trump at the White House later this week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Kenyan premier Raila Odinga, a key figure in African democracy efforts, dies at 80
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Raila Odinga, a former prime minister of Kenya and perennial presidential candidate whose populist campaigns challenged one-party rule, rattled authorities and gave him outsized influence on political life in the East African country, died Wednesday of a heart attack while trav
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Remains released by Hamas not a hostage, Israel's military says
item.description: The original framework of a U.S.-brokered ceasefire required Hamas to return all 28 deceased hostages Monday.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Russia using new, more precise drone tech to attack Ukraine’s vital rail network
item.description: Since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion in early 2022, railway officials have publicly reported about roughly one attack on railways per week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American who was on death row in China details horrifying experiences
item.description: Speaking in his first television interview since being released in November 2024 as part of a prisoner swap, Swidan detailed his experience on "Elizabeth Vargas Reports.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Russian aerial attack hits a Ukrainian hospital, days before Zelenskyy meets Trump
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched powerful glide bombs and drones against Ukraine’s second-largest city in overnight attacks, hitting a hospital and wounding seven people, an official said Tuesday, as European military aid for Kyiv dropped sharply and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensk
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump meets with Argentina's president on tariffs, bailout
item.description: The U.S. recently announced a currency exchange of $20 billion in pesos for an equal amount of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Russia may change law, call up 2 million reservists to fight in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW, Oct 14 (Reuters) - Russia will be able to deploy around two million military reservists to fight in Ukraine if needed under amendments to the law likely to be backed by parliament, giving it greater flexibility to ramp up the size of its force there.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Osborn, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: No end in sight to government shutdown as House speaker refuses to negotiate
item.description: The shutdown entered its 14th day with the Senate returning from its holiday break, Republican House Speaker Mike Johnson refusing to call lawmakers back to Washington and President Donald Trump’s budget director vowing to keep firing federal workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas holds public executions, tightens grip on Gaza
item.description: CAIRO/JERUSALEM, Oct 14 (Reuters) - Israel restricted aid into Gaza and kept the enclave's border shut on Tuesday while re-emergent Hamas fighters demonstrated their grip by executing men in the street, darkening the outlook for U.S. President Donald Trump's plan to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Nidal al-Mughrabi and Maayan Lubell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's power worldwide has 'increased 1,000%': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Sunday marked 737 days since the hostages were taken, and all 20 hostages remaining alive were released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Living hostages and Palestinian prisoners freed as Trump tours region
item.description: Hamas released all 20 of the last living hostages on Monday, the Israeli military said, as part of a breakthrough ceasefire after two years of war between Israel and Hamas in the devastated Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel will exile most released Palestinian prisoners with life sentences
item.description: At least 154 prisoners, most of whom have been convicted for attacks on Israelis, will be deported, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to visit Washington this week seeking long-range weapons and a Trump meeting
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Monday he will travel to the United States this week for talks on the potential U.S. provision of long-range weapons, a day after U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump to Knesset: Israel has won ‘all that they can’ in military campaign
item.description: President Trump on Monday urged Israeli leaders to turn the page from warfare to peace, telling the Knesset that Israel had won “all that they can by force of arms” and expressing optimism about the region’s future.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump warns Russia he may send Ukraine long-range Tomahawks if Moscow doesn't settle war soon
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump on Sunday warned Russia that he may send Ukraine long-range Tomahawk missiles if Moscow doesn’t settle its war there soon — suggesting that he could be ready to increase the pressure on Vladimir Putin's government using a key weapons system.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9
